title: Channel Model Turn Baseline
objective: Install the deterministic Kova channel, run real inbound channel events through the OpenClaw agent/model path with a mock provider, and verify channel sends, receipts, timing, duplicate-send behavior, and terminal returns per model-turn case.

phases.title: Provision Channel Model Turn Env
phases.intent: Create a disposable OpenClaw environment without starting the gateway so Kova mock auth and the test channel plugin are installed before plugin discovery.

phases.title: Install Kova Channel Plugin
phases.intent: Install the deterministic Kova channel plugin through the user-visible plugin install path before gateway startup.

phases.title: Start Gateway With Kova Channel
phases.intent: Start the OpenClaw gateway after mock auth and plugin installation so the channel and model provider are discovered through the normal runtime path.

phases.title: Run Text Final Model Turn
phases.intent: Call the Kova channel plugin over Gateway RPC so OpenClaw handles one inbound text-final channel event, calls the mock provider, sends a final text response through the channel adapter, and records reply/hook/terminal invariants.
evidence: provider request policy and observed count

phases.title: Run Media Final Model Turn
phases.intent: Call the Kova channel plugin over Gateway RPC so OpenClaw handles one inbound media-final channel event, calls the mock provider, stages local media for outbound delivery, sends it through the channel adapter, and records receipt, duplicate, and terminal invariants.
evidence: provider request policy and observed count

phases.title: Run Media Thread Final Model Turn
phases.intent: Call the Kova channel plugin over Gateway RPC so OpenClaw handles one inbound threaded media request, calls the mock provider, sends media through the channel adapter in that thread, and records media/thread/receipt/terminal invariants.
evidence: media thread final model-turn capability rows
provider request policy and observed count

phases.title: Run Media Reply Thread Final Model Turn
phases.intent: Call the Kova channel plugin over Gateway RPC so OpenClaw handles one inbound threaded reply media request, calls the mock provider, sends media through the channel adapter against the right reply and thread targets, and records media/reply/thread/receipt/terminal invariants.
evidence: media reply thread final model-turn capability rows
provider request policy and observed count

phases.title: Run Source Delivery Model Turn
phases.intent: Call the Kova channel plugin over Gateway RPC so OpenClaw handles one normal inbound user event, follows its supported source-delivery contract, sends visible source output through the message tool when required, and records payload, receipt, duplicate-send, and terminal invariants.
evidence: user action and inbound channel event ids
provider request policy and observed count

phases.title: Run Background Artifact Completion Model Turn
phases.intent: Call the Kova channel plugin over Gateway RPC so OpenClaw handles one normal media-generation completion shape, follows its supported message-tool source delivery contract, and records artifact, receipt, duplicate-send, and terminal invariants.
evidence: user action and inbound channel event ids
provider request policy and observed count

phases.title: Run Thread Final Model Turn
phases.intent: Call the Kova channel plugin over Gateway RPC so OpenClaw handles one inbound threaded channel event, calls the mock provider, sends a final response through the channel adapter, and records thread preservation and terminal invariants.
evidence: provider request policy and observed count

phases.title: Run Silent Final Model Turn
phases.intent: Call the Kova channel plugin over Gateway RPC so OpenClaw handles one inbound silent-final channel event, calls the mock provider, sends a final response through the channel adapter, and records silent delivery and terminal invariants.
evidence: provider request policy and observed count

phases.title: Run Negative Path Model Turns
phases.intent: Call the Kova channel plugin over Gateway RPC so OpenClaw handles a normal inbound channel event while the model provider fails, sends one visible error response through the channel adapter, and records duplicate-send and terminal invariants.

phases.title: Post-Channel-Model-Turn Health
phases.intent: Verify the gateway remains responsive after the channel model turn.
